title: Recover a Restarted Robot That Does Not Reappear on the Map
summary: Recover a robot that was restarted while stopped between labels and does not automatically reappear on the map because it cannot identify a label. The source describes confirming the condition, observing the flashing blue state, E-stopping the system, entering the floor, pushing the robot onto a label, waiting for the robot to lock onto the label, clearing the E-stop, and verifying the robot appears on the map and changes from flashing blue to solid blue.
when_to_use: Use when a robot has been restarted while stopped between two labels, does not snap back onto the map automatically, and the source-described condition indicates it cannot see a label.
not_for: Do not use before the restart cycle reaches the green state if the robot still has locked brakes; the source states to wait until it turns green before moving it because the brakes stay locked until then.
Do not use as a general operator procedure without site-approved controls; this recovery includes E-stop use, floor entry, and physically moving the robot.
Do not use for cases where the robot is already positioned at a restart point or QR code cell and displays normally on the map after restart.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Confirm the restart/map visibility condition, apply the system E-stop, enter the floor, physically reposition the robot onto a label, observe label lock behavior, clear the E-stop, and verify map recovery.

safety_notes: This procedure is not support-safe for general operator execution based on the source packet.
The procedure includes use of the system E-stop before entering the floor.
The procedure includes entering the floor and physically pushing the robot.
The source states to wait until the robot turns green before moving it because the brakes stay locked until then.
The source also notes the software E-stop pauses the system but is not a physical emergency stop and robots in motion may continue to the next label before stopping.
access_or_tools_needed: Ability to physically push the robot
Visual access to robot color/state indication

access_required: Access to the system E-stop control
Authorized physical access to the floor
system_preconditions: The robot is stopped between labels and cannot identify a label.
The robot does not automatically reappear on the map after restart.
Wait until the robot reaches the green state before moving it because the brakes stay locked until then.

visual_references.description: Training video frame and transcript segment covering restart behavior, flashing blue indication, label reacquisition, brake lock behavior, and successful map recovery.

steps.title: Confirm the robot is not back on the map after restart
steps.purpose: Verify that the source-described recovery condition exists before taking physical recovery actions.
steps.instruction: Confirm that after the restart the robot is not showing on the map as expected and that it cannot see a label.
steps.expected_result: The affected robot is confirmed to have not snapped back onto the map automatically after restart because it cannot identify a label.
steps.healthy_condition: The condition matches the source description of a restarted robot stopped between labels and not displayed correctly on the map.
steps.failure_condition: The robot is already visible on the map or the source-described between-label/no-label condition cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Check map visibility
actions.exact_action: Confirm that after restart the robot is not showing on the map as expected and that it cannot see a label.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Restart guidance indicating the robot may not be displayed on the map after restart unless positioned at a restart point or QR code cell.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the robot is already showing normally on the map.
Escalate if the observed condition does not match the source-described restart-between-labels scenario.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'if your robot is stopped for some reason in between two labels and you perform a restart, it will not snap back onto the map automatically... It's because it can't see a label.'

steps.title: Observe the flashing blue robot indication
steps.purpose: Use the source-described visual state to confirm the robot cannot see a label.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the robot shows the flashing blue indication mentioned in the source.
steps.expected_result: The robot shows the flashing blue indication associated with not seeing a label.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot is flashing blue, matching the source-described state for this condition.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not show the flashing blue indication or its state cannot be verified.

actions.label: Check robot indicator state
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the robot shows the flashing blue indication mentioned in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training segment describing the flashing blue sign when the robot cannot see a label after restart.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the robot state does not match the source-described flashing blue condition.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'you'll see that flashing blue sign.'

steps.title: E-stop the system before entering the floor
steps.purpose: Pause the system before floor entry and physical interaction with the robot, as described in the source.
steps.instruction: E-stop the system before entering the floor, as described in the training segment.
steps.expected_result: The system is E-stopped before floor entry.
steps.healthy_condition: The E-stop has been applied before entering the floor.
steps.failure_condition: The system is not E-stopped before floor entry.

actions.label: Apply system E-stop
actions.exact_action: E-stop the system before entering the floor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Map monitor summary area showing the system E-stop control.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if floor entry would occur before the system is E-stopped.
Escalate if site controls require additional authorization not provided in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'you will have to E stop the system, walk into the floor...'
The map monitor artifact identifies the system E-stop control and notes the software E-stop pauses the system but is not a physical emergency stop.

steps.title: Enter the floor and push the robot onto a label
steps.purpose: Reposition the robot so it can identify a label and recover map position.
steps.instruction: Walk into the floor and push the robot onto the label.
steps.expected_result: The robot is physically repositioned onto a label.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot is placed onto a label so it can identify it.
steps.failure_condition: The robot is not on a label or cannot be repositioned onto one.

actions.label: Push robot onto label
actions.exact_action: Walk into the floor and push the robot onto the label.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The recovery description stating to walk into the floor and push the robot onto the label.

stop_or_escalate_if: Do not move the robot before the source-described restart cycle reaches the green state because the brakes stay locked until then.
Escalate if the robot cannot be physically moved onto a label.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'you will have to E stop the system, walk into the floor, push the robot onto the label...'
Transcript: 'you have to wait till it turns green for you to move it because the brakes stay locked.'

steps.title: Wait for the robot to identify and lock onto the label
steps.purpose: Confirm the robot has reacquired a valid label reference before clearing the E-stop.
steps.instruction: Watch for the robot to identify the label and lock itself on the label; the source states this brake-lock behavior is how you know it has identified the label.
steps.expected_result: The robot identifies the label and locks itself on the label.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot locks itself on the label, indicating successful label identification.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not lock onto the label after being pushed onto it.

actions.label: Observe label lock behavior
actions.exact_action: Watch for the robot to identify the label and lock itself on the label.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The transcript statement that the robot will lock itself on the label when it identifies it.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the robot does not lock onto the label after being pushed onto it.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'it will lock itself on the label' and this is how you know it has identified the label.

steps.title: Exit the floor and clear the E-stop
steps.purpose: Return the system from the paused state after the robot has reacquired the label.
steps.instruction: Exit the floor and clear the E-stop.
steps.expected_result: The floor is exited and the E-stop is cleared.
steps.healthy_condition: The E-stop is cleared only after the robot has locked onto the label and personnel have exited the floor.
steps.failure_condition: The E-stop cannot be cleared or is cleared before the robot has locked onto the label and floor exit is complete.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System E-stop control in the map monitor summary area.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the robot has not yet locked onto the label.
Escalate if the E-stop cannot be cleared or the system does not resume as expected.

steps.title: Verify the robot appears successfully on the map
steps.purpose: Confirm the recovery restored map visibility.
steps.instruction: Verify that the robot shows successfully on the map.
steps.expected_result: The robot appears successfully on the map.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot is shown successfully on the map after the E-stop is cleared.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not appear successfully on the map after the E-stop is cleared.

actions.label: Verify map recovery
actions.exact_action: Verify that the robot shows successfully on the map.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The restart guidance stating the robot will be shown successfully on the map after recovery.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the robot does not appear successfully on the map after the E-stop is cleared.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'you'll see the robot being successfully showing successful on the map itself...'

steps.title: Confirm the robot changes from flashing blue to solid blue
steps.purpose: Use the source-described color change as a final visual confirmation of label pickup and recovery progress.
steps.instruction: Confirm the color change described in the source from flashing blue to solid blue after label pickup.
steps.expected_result: The robot changes from flashing blue to solid blue as described in the source.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot is solid blue after label pickup.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not change from flashing blue to solid blue.

actions.label: Verify color state change
actions.exact_action: Confirm the color change described in the source from flashing blue to solid blue after label pickup.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training segment describing the robot going from flashing blue to solid blue after label pickup.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the robot does not change from flashing blue to solid blue after label pickup.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'it will go from flashing blue to solid blue.'

success_criteria: The robot locks itself on the label.
The robot appears successfully on the map after the E-stop is cleared.
The robot changes from flashing blue to solid blue as described in the source.
healthy_conditions: The robot is confirmed to have been in the source-described between-label restart condition.
The robot shows the flashing blue indication before recovery.
The robot locks onto the label after being pushed onto it.
The robot is shown successfully on the map after recovery.
The robot changes from flashing blue to solid blue.
failure_conditions: The robot does not lock onto the label after being pushed onto it.
The robot does not appear successfully on the map after the E-stop is cleared.
The robot does not change from flashing blue to solid blue.
The robot is moved before the restart cycle reaches the green state while brakes are still locked.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the robot does not lock onto the label after being pushed onto it.
Escalate if the robot does not appear successfully on the map after the E-stop is cleared.
Escalate if the robot state does not match the source-described flashing blue / solid blue recovery pattern.
Escalate if site-approved floor-entry or safety controls required for this action are not available in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary source artifact for restart recovery, flashing blue state, label reacquisition, brake lock behavior, and map recovery.
Map monitor summary area showing the system E-stop control referenced for this recovery.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'if your robot is stopped for some reason in between two labels and you perform a restart, it will not snap back onto the map automatically... It's because it can't see a label and you'll see that flashing blue sign. In that case... you will have to E stop the system, walk into the floor, push the robot onto the label... it will lock itself on the label... clear the E stop and you'll see the robot being successfully showing successful on the map itself... it will go from flashing blue to solid blue.'

missing_details: The source does not provide a detailed guarded-area entry procedure or authorization workflow for entering the floor.
The source does not specify whether a physical emergency stop, lockout/tagout, or additional site safety controls are required.
The source does not provide a precise map-screen workflow for locating the affected robot during this recovery.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing the recovery.
The source does not provide additional troubleshooting steps if the robot still fails to identify the label after repositioning.
